main_title: 3 Unsettling Stories of College Students Who Were Scammed by Fake Online Marketplaces

stories.seed: I thought I'd found an amazing laptop deal through a Facebook Marketplace seller, but when I met him at the campus coffee shop to exchange cash, he seemed overly interested in my class schedule. As I walked back to my dorm, I realized he'd been casually asking me questions about my alone time and daily habits, and now I'm convinced he's been watching me ever since.
stories.summary: A college student's attempt to buy a laptop online turns into a stalking nightmare when the seller becomes obsessed with their daily routine.

stories.seed: I booked a ride through a sketchy online carpooling service to get to my part-time job at a restaurant on the outskirts of town, and the driver, who claimed to be a fellow student, took a detour down a deserted road, saying his GPS was malfunctioning. As we drove further into the woods, I realized his ID didn't match the one on the service's website, and he started asking me invasive questions about my financial situation.
stories.summary: A college student's daily commute turns into a terrifying ordeal when their ride share driver takes a detour into the woods with sinister intentions.

stories.seed: I responded to an ad on a college forum to house sit for a professor over winter break, but when I arrived at the mansion, I discovered it was actually a group of strangers running some kind of fake rental scam, and they expected me to stay and "watch the house" in exchange for a place to stay. As I tried to leave, they became aggressive, insisting I'd signed a contract and owed them money, and now I'm trapped in this huge, creepy house with no phone signal.
stories.summary: A college student falls prey to a house sitting scam and finds themselves trapped in a strange mansion with a group of hostile strangers who refuse to let them leave.
